topic_title: Agent anatomy — LLM plus memory, tools, and a planning loop
verdict: PASS (second pass) — scope_creep resolved. Both ReAct mentions are now bare name+pointer only: no bold, no defining clause, no appositive. All 6 checks clear. One soft readability finding surfaced for HITL #2 awareness; does not escalate to hard fail (1 finding, threshold is 3).

findings.exact_text_excerpt: Those pieces are: memory (so the agent can remember things across steps), tools (so the agent can take actions in the world), and a planning loop (so the agent can figure out what to do next...)
findings.detail: The introductory paragraph introduces three new terms in a single sentence as a preview list with brief inline glosses. Each is fully defined individually in section 5.2. This is a recognised preview-then-define pattern; the inline glosses are minimal (one-clause each). Borderline jargon_dump rather than a clear violation. Surfaced for author awareness at HITL #2 only — not a hard fail.
findings.author_option: Optional: split into three short sentences or use a numbered list to avoid any appearance of term-stacking.

notes: All primary terms defined on first use with bold + plain-English meaning: AI agent (sections 4 and 5.1), LLM core (section 5.2), short-term memory (section 5.2), long-term memory (section 5.2), tools (section 5.2), planning loop (section 5.2). PASS.
context window defined inline at section 5.2: 'the maximum amount of text the model can process at once'. PASS.
LLM full form carried over from earlier M5 topics per Prerequisites section. API likewise. PASS.

Concrete analogies used throughout: 'desk' for short-term memory, 'filing cabinet' for long-term memory, 'brain / hands / legs / filing cabinet' for components, 'small team' for the agent system. PASS.
No hedging phrases ('it is generally considered', 'many believe') found in corpus. PASS.
Average sentence length well under 25 words across sampled sections. Active voice. PASS.
1 soft finding only — below the ≥3 threshold for escalation to hard fail.
